subject: unravel_index dims keyword argument
old: Passing the shape argument as the 'dims' keyword argument to unravel_index
truth: Stop passing the shape argument to unravel_index as the 'dims' keyword argument; use the positional 'shape' argument instead.
quote: The shape argument ~numpy.unravel_index cannot be passed as dims keyword argument anymore. (Was deprecated in NumPy 1.16.)

from_fact: passing the shape argument as the 'dims' keyword argument to unravel_index
associative: how to pass shape to unravel_index

subject: PyUFunc_GenericFunction
old: Calling PyUFunc_GenericFunction C API function
truth: Stop using PyUFunc_GenericFunction; call the ufunc directly using the Python API instead.
quote: The function PyUFunc_GenericFunction has been disabled. It was deprecated in NumPy 1.19. Users should call the ufunc directly using the Python API.

subject: PyUFunc_SetUsesArraysAsData
old: Calling PyUFunc_SetUsesArraysAsData C API function
truth: Stop using PyUFunc_SetUsesArraysAsData; it has been disabled.
quote: The function PyUFunc_SetUsesArraysAsData has been disabled. It was deprecated in NumPy 1.19.

subject: PolyBase class
old: Using the PolyBase class from numpy.polynomial
truth: Stop using PolyBase; use ABCPolyBase instead.
quote: The class PolyBase has been removed (deprecated in numpy 1.9.0). Please use the abstract ABCPolyBase class instead.

from_fact: using the polybase class from numpy.polynomial

subject: PolyError and PolyDomainError exceptions
old: Catching or raising PolyError or PolyDomainError from numpy.polynomial
truth: Stop using PolyError and PolyDomainError; they have been removed.
quote: The unused PolyError and PolyDomainError exceptions are removed.

from_fact: catching or raising polyerror or polydomainerror from numpy.polynomial

subject: array-like creation with objects raising exceptions on __array__ or __array_interface__
old: exceptions (other than AttributeError) during __array__ or __array_interface__ access were silently ignored
new: a warning is now given when the exception is anything but AttributeError
truth: If an object raises a non-AttributeError exception during __array__ or __array_interface__ access, a warning is now emitted; adapt the type to raise AttributeError to silence the warning.
quote: A warning is now given when the exception is anything but AttributeError.

from_fact: exceptions (other than attributeerror) during __array__ or __array_interface__ access were silently ignored
a warning is now given when the exception is anything but attributeerror

subject: PolyError and PolyDomainError exceptions
old: numpy.polynomial.PolyError and numpy.polynomial.PolyDomainError
truth: PolyError and PolyDomainError have been removed from numpy.polynomial.
quote: removed from the `numpy.polynomial`.

subject: universal functions error types
old: RuntimeError raised on invalid input in some cases
new: TypeError raised instead of RuntimeError on invalid input in some cases
truth: Universal functions may now raise TypeError instead of RuntimeError on invalid input; the specific error raised when multiple errors are present may also differ.
quote: The universal functions may now raise different errors on invalid input in some

from_fact: runtimeerror raised on invalid input in some cases
typeerror raised instead of runtimeerror on invalid input in some cases

subject: __array_ufunc__ argument validation
old: invalid arguments (e.g. non-existing keyword) could be passed through when dispatch was known to occur
new: NumPy now partially validates arguments before calling __array_ufunc__
truth: NumPy now validates arguments before calling __array_ufunc__, so previously-passable invalid arguments like non-existing keywords will now be rejected.
quote: non-existing keyword argument) when dispatch was known to occur.

from_fact: invalid arguments (e.g. non-existing keyword) could be passed through when dispatch was known to occur
numpy now partially validates arguments before calling __array_ufunc__

subject: __array_ufunc__ dispatch for positional arguments in reduce/accumulate/reduceat
old: all positionally passed arguments were checked for __array_ufunc__ support, so e.g. the `where` array in np.add.reduce could be dispatched on
new: NumPy only dispatches on the input and output array; positional arguments like `where` in reductions are never dispatched on
truth: NumPy no longer dispatches __array_ufunc__ on positional arguments other than input/output arrays in reduce, accumulate, and reduceat; e.g. the `where` array in np.add.reduce will never be dispatched on.
quote: Previously, all positionally passed arguments were checked for

from_fact: all positionally passed arguments were checked for __array_ufunc__ support, so e.g. the `where` array in np.add.reduce could be dispatched on
numpy only dispatches on the input and output array; positional arguments like `where` in reductions are never dispatched on

subject: np.random.Generator.uniform input validation
old: out-of-order inputs (low > high) were silently accepted and swapped, generating high + (low - high) * random()
new: raises ValueError if low > high (high - low >= 0 is required)
truth: np.random.Generator.uniform now raises ValueError if low > high; previously out-of-order inputs were silently swapped.
quote: Previously out-of-order inputs were accepted

from_fact: out-of-order inputs (low > high) were silently accepted and swapped, generating high + (low - high) * random()
raises valueerror if low > high (high - low >= 0 is required)

subject: dtype and signature arguments in ufunc reductions (np.add.reduce, np.sum)
old: NumPy almost always ignored metadata, byteorder or time units on dtype/signature inputs; some cases silently used the info
new: NumPy now always ignores metadata/byteorder/time units and raises an error if byteorder or time unit changed
truth: Using dtype or signature in ufunc reductions (e.g. np.add.reduce) now raises an error if byteorder or time unit is specified and changed; previously such info was often silently ignored.
quote: NumPy almost always ignored metadata, byteorder or time units

from_fact: numpy almost always ignored metadata, byteorder or time units on dtype/signature inputs; some cases silently used the info
numpy now always ignores metadata/byteorder/time units and raises an error if byteorder or time unit changed

subject: np.add dtype parameter with byte-order specifier
old: dtype='>i32' was previously ignored (byte-order silently ignored)
new: byte-order specifier in dtype is now respected
truth: Stop relying on dtype byte-order specifiers being ignored; they are now honored and may change behavior for non-native byte orders.
quote: Previously ignored the byte-order (affect if non-native)

from_fact: dtype='>i32' was previously ignored (byte-order silently ignored)
byte-order specifier in dtype is now respected

subject: np.add dtype with timedelta64 unit specifier
old: dtype='m8[ns]' on array inputs previously ignored the unit and used the array's unit
new: dtype='m8[ns]' on array inputs now uses the array's unit (e.g. 's') instead of the specified unit
truth: Stop relying on dtype='m8[ns]' overriding the input array's time unit; the array's unit now takes precedence.
quote: # The examples always ignored the time unit "ns":

from_fact: dtype='m8[ns]' on array inputs previously ignored the unit and used the array's unit
dtype='m8[ns]' on array inputs now uses the array's unit (e.g. 's') instead of the specified unit

subject: np.add with dtype='m8[ns]' on scalar inputs
old: np.add(3, 5, dtype='m8[ns]') previously returned timedelta64 with ns unit
new: np.add(3, 5, dtype='m8[ns]') now returns generic timedelta64 without unit
truth: Stop relying on dtype='m8[ns]' on scalar inputs producing ns-precision timedelta64; it now returns a generic timedelta64.
quote: np.add(3, 5, dtype="m8[ns]")  # Now return generic time units

from_fact: np.add(3, 5, dtype='m8[ns]') previously returned timedelta64 with ns unit
np.add(3, 5, dtype='m8[ns]') now returns generic timedelta64 without unit

subject: np.maximum with dtype='m8[ns]'
old: np.maximum(arr, arr, dtype='m8[ns]') previously used ns unit
new: np.maximum(arr, arr, dtype='m8[ns]') now returns the array's unit (e.g. 's')
truth: Stop relying on dtype='m8[ns]' in np.maximum overriding the input array's time unit; the array's unit is now used.
quote: np.maximum(arr, arr, dtype="m8[ns]")  # Now returns "s" (from `arr`)

from_fact: np.maximum(arr, arr, dtype='m8[ns]') previously used ns unit
np.maximum(arr, arr, dtype='m8[ns]') now returns the array's unit (e.g. 's')

subject: np.sum and similar functions with timedelta64 dtype
old: np.sum and similar functions previously used the specified dtype time unit
new: np.sum and similar functions now use the input array's time unit instead of the specified dtype unit
truth: Stop relying on np.sum (and similar reduction functions) with dtype='m8[ns]' overriding the input's time unit; the input's unit now takes precedence.
quote: The universal functions may now raise different errors on invalid input in some

from_fact: np.sum and similar functions previously used the specified dtype time unit
np.sum and similar functions now use the input array's time unit instead of the specified dtype unit

subject: ufunc dtype parameter meaning
old: dtype='float64' previously enforced both input and output dtypes
new: dtype='float64' now strictly enforces only the output dtype (equivalent to signature=(None, None, 'float64'))
truth: Stop relying on dtype='float64' in ufuncs enforcing the input dtype; it now only enforces the output dtype and may fail to find a loop if inputs don't match.
quote: now strictly enforces only the correct output (and not input) DTypes.

from_fact: dtype='float64' previously enforced both input and output dtypes
dtype='float64' now strictly enforces only the output dtype (equivalent to signature=(none, none, 'float64'))

subject: Generator.rayleigh and Generator.geometric random variate stream
old: log-based inverse cdf transformation
new: Ziggurat-based exponential variate generator
truth: Generator.rayleigh and Generator.geometric now produce a different stream of random variates than before, breaking reproducibility of sequences generated with the same seed.
quote: This change breaks the stream of variates generated when variates from either of these distributions are produced.

subject: np.core.MachAr
old: np.core.MachAr
truth: Stop using np.core.MachAr; it is deprecated private API.
quote: np.core.MachAr is deprecated. It is private API.

subject: np.finfo(None)
old: np.finfo(None)
truth: Stop calling np.finfo(None); it is deprecated.
quote: np.finfo(None) is deprecated.

subject: np.round_
old: np.round_
new: np.round
truth: Stop using np.round_; use np.round instead.
quote: np.round_ is deprecated. Use np.round instead.

subject: np.product
old: np.product
new: np.prod
truth: Stop using np.product; use np.prod instead.
quote: np.product is deprecated. Use np.prod instead.

subject: np.cumproduct
old: np.cumproduct
new: np.cumprod
truth: Stop using np.cumproduct; use np.cumprod instead.
quote: np.cumproduct is deprecated. Use np.cumprod instead.

subject: np.sometrue
old: np.sometrue
new: np.any
truth: Stop using np.sometrue; use np.any instead.
quote: np.sometrue is deprecated. Use np.any instead.

subject: np.alltrue
old: np.alltrue
new: np.all
truth: Stop using np.alltrue; use np.all instead.
quote: np.alltrue is deprecated. Use np.all instead.

subject: scalar treatment of size-1 arrays
old: All arrays of size 1 (e.g. np.array([3.14])) treated as scalars
new: Only ndim-0 arrays treated as scalars
truth: Stop relying on size-1 arrays (e.g. np.array([3.14])) being treated as scalars; only ndim-0 arrays (e.g. np.array(3.14)) are scalars now.
quote: Only ndim-0 arrays are treated as scalars. NumPy used to treat all arrays of size 1 (e.g., np.array([3.14])) as scalars. In the future, this will be limited to arrays of ndim 0 (e.g., np.array(3.14)).

from_fact: all arrays of size 1 (e.g. np.array([3.14])) treated as scalars
only ndim-0 arrays treated as scalars

subject: np.find_common_type
old: np.find_common_type
new: np.result_type or np.promote_types
truth: Stop using np.find_common_type; use np.result_type or np.promote_types instead.
quote: with either `numpy.result_type` or `numpy.promote_types`.

subject: np.core.machar
old: np.core.machar
truth: Stop using np.core.machar and np.finfo.machar as they have been removed.
quote: np.core.machar and np.finfo.machar have been removed.

subject: unary plus operator on non-numeric arrays
old: +arr on non-numeric dtype silently worked or did nothing
new: +arr raises an error when dtype is not numeric
truth: Using +arr on arrays with non-numeric dtype now raises an error instead of succeeding.
quote: +arr will now raise an error when the dtype is not numeric (and positive is undefined).

from_fact: +arr on non-numeric dtype silently worked or did nothing
+arr raises an error when dtype is not numeric

subject: stacking functions argument
old: stack, vstack, hstack, dstack, column_stack accepted a variable number of positional arguments
new: these functions now require a sequence (list/tuple) argument
truth: Stop passing multiple positional arguments to stack/vstack/hstack/dstack/column_stack; pass a single sequence instead.
quote: A sequence must now be passed into the stacking family of functions (stack, vstack, hstack, dstack and column_stack).

from_fact: stack, vstack, hstack, dstack, column_stack accepted a variable number of positional arguments
these functions now require a sequence (list/tuple) argument

subject: np.clip casting behavior
old: np.clip fell back to unsafe casting
new: np.clip now defaults to same-kind casting
truth: np.clip now uses same-kind casting by default instead of unsafe casting, which may raise errors where it previously succeeded.
quote: np.clip now defaults to same-kind casting. Falling back to unsafe casting was deprecated in NumPy 1.17.

from_fact: np.clip fell back to unsafe casting
np.clip now defaults to same-kind casting

subject: np.clip NaN propagation
old: scalar NaN passed as min/max to np.clip was usually ignored
new: np.clip now propagates NaN values passed as min or max
truth: Passing NaN as min or max to np.clip now propagates the NaN in the output instead of ignoring it.
quote: np.clip will now propagate np.nan values passed as min or max. Previously, a scalar NaN was usually ignored.

from_fact: scalar nan passed as min/max to np.clip was usually ignored
np.clip now propagates nan values passed as min or max

subject: np.dual submodule
old: np.dual
truth: Stop using np.dual as it has been removed.
quote: The np.dual submodule has been removed.

subject: sequence behavior for array-like objects
old: array-like objects defining array protocols could also be treated as sequences
new: NumPy now always ignores sequence behavior for array-likes that define array protocols
truth: Array-like objects that define array protocols will no longer be treated as sequences; only the protocol is used.
quote: NumPy now always ignores sequence behavior for an array-like (defining one of the array protocols).

from_fact: array-like objects defining array protocols could also be treated as sequences
numpy now always ignores sequence behavior for array-likes that define array protocols

subject: casting to subarray dtype in astype/asarray
old: casting to subarray dtype sometimes behaved differently (with FutureWarning)
new: behavior is now always the same as wrapping subarray dtype into a single field
truth: Casting to a subarray dtype in astype, asarray, etc. now always behaves as if the subarray dtype was wrapped into a single field.
quote: The behavior is now always the same as if the subarray dtype was wrapped into a single field (which was the workaround, previously).

from_fact: casting to subarray dtype sometimes behaved differently (with futurewarning)
behavior is now always the same as wrapping subarray dtype into a single field

subject: == and != operators on arrays with incompatible shapes
old: comparing arrays with incompatible shapes gave a warning
new: comparing arrays with incompatible shapes now raises an error
truth: Using == or != on arrays with incompatible shapes now raises an error instead of a warning.
quote: raise errors that occur during comparisons such as when the arrays have incompatible shapes (np.array([1, 2]) == np.array([1, 2, 3]))

from_fact: comparing arrays with incompatible shapes gave a warning
comparing arrays with incompatible shapes now raises an error

subject: == and != operators on arrays with incompatible dtypes
old: comparing arrays with fundamentally incompatible dtypes gave a warning
new: returns array of all True or all False (mimics Python behavior)
truth: Using == or != on arrays with incompatible dtypes now returns an array of all True or all False instead of warning.
quote: return an array of all True or all False when values are fundamentally not comparable (e.g. have different dtypes). An example is np.array(["a"]) == np.array([1])

from_fact: comparing arrays with fundamentally incompatible dtypes gave a warning
returns array of all true or all false (mimics python behavior)

subject: Nose testing support
old: numpy's nose-based test decorators (raises, slow, setastest, skipif, knownfailif, deprecated, parametrize, _needs_refcount) and functions (Tester, import_nose, run_module_suite)
truth: Stop using numpy's nose test decorators and functions; use pytest equivalents instead.

new: np.power(0, non-zero complex) now returns 0+0j or 0-0j
truth: np.power of complex zero to a non-zero exponent now returns 0+0j or 0-0j instead of NaN
quote: Previously, NaN was returned unless the imaginary part was strictly

from_fact: np.power(0, non-zero complex) returned nan unless imaginary part was strictly zero
np.power(0, non-zero complex) now returns 0+0j or 0-0j

old: saved dtype metadata in .npy/.npz files, causing read failures
new: drop dtype metadata before saving

quote: Now, `np.save` and `np.savez` drop metadata before saving.

subject: uint64 and int64 comparisons
old: promoted both to float64, giving incorrect comparison results
new: avoid cast to float, returning correct comparison results
truth: uint64 and int64 comparisons now return correct results instead of promoting to float64 which gave incorrect booleans
quote: NumPy will now return the correct results for these by avoiding the cast to float.

from_fact: promoted both to float64, giving incorrect comparison results
avoid cast to float, returning correct comparison results

old: np.r_[np.arange(5, dtype=np.uint8), -1] returned default integer dtype (int64 or int32)
new: np.r_[np.arange(5, dtype=np.uint8), -1] returns int16 dtype

np.r_[np.arange(5, dtype=np.uint8), -1].dtype
int16  # rather than the default integer (int64 or int32)

old: dtype=... in comparison ufuncs did not denote output dtype; np.equal(2, 3, dtype=object) did not return object array

quote: ufuncs (``equal``, ``less``, etc.) is used, this will denote

from_fact: dtype=... in comparison ufuncs did not denote output dtype; np.equal(2, 3, dtype=object) did not return object array
dtype= (or signature) in comparison ufuncs will denote the desired output dtype; using non-bool dtype gives futurewarning/deprecationwarning and will error in future

subject: ufunc signature parameter casting behavior
old: signature=... relaxed casting checks on inputs, potentially allowing unsafe downcasting
new: signature=... now guarantees safe casting; partial signatures may cause no loop found error

subject: NumPy distutils clang floating point model
old: clang compiler did not have -ffp-exception-behavior=strict forced
new: NumPy distutils now always adds -ffp-exception-behavior=strict when compiling with clang
truth: Compiling NumPy extensions with clang now forces strict floating point exception behavior, which may change code generation and floating point warning/error behavior.

new: calling ufunc->type_resolver or PyUFunc_DefaultTypeResolver now enforces a normalized type tuple

old: busday_count included enddates when begindates was later than enddates

new: np.equal and np.not_equal now fail when comparing datetime and timedelta with casting='unsafe'

old: np.load raised ValueError (allow_pickle=False) or OSError (allow_pickle=True) when file handle was at end of file
new: np.load now raises EOFError in both cases when file handle is at end of file
truth: Catch EOFError instead of ValueError or OSError when np.load reads from a file handle already at end of file.
quote: raised ``ValueError`` if ``allow_pickle=False``, and ``OSError`` if

from_fact: np.load raised valueerror (allow_pickle=false) or oserror (allow_pickle=true) when file handle was at end of file
np.load now raises eoferror in both cases when file handle is at end of file

subject: np.pad with mode='wrap' for padding larger than array
old: np.pad with mode='wrap' did not use strict multiples of original data when padding size exceeded array size
new: np.pad with mode='wrap' now always pads with strict multiples of original data
truth: np.pad with mode='wrap' now produces different results when padding size is larger than the array; it uses strict multiples of the original data.
quote: ``np.pad`` with ``mode=wrap`` pads with strict multiples of original data

from_fact: np.pad with mode='wrap' did not use strict multiples of original data when padding size exceeded array size
np.pad with mode='wrap' now always pads with strict multiples of original data

subject: Cython type aliases long_t and ulong_t
old: long_t and ulong_t
truth: Stop using Cython long_t and ulong_t; use cnp.int64_t, cnp.intp_t, plain long, or npy_long instead.
quote: Cython ``long_t`` and ``ulong_t`` removed

associative: what to use instead of long_t

subject: Error type for bad axes argument to ufunc
old: TypeError was raised for mismatched axes values
new: AxisError is now raised for mismatched axes values (TypeError still raised for invalid types)
truth: Code catching TypeError for mismatched axes values in ufunc(..., axes=[...]) will no longer catch the error; catch AxisError instead.
quote: A ``TypeError`` will still be raised for invalid input types.

from_fact: typeerror was raised for mismatched axes values
axiserror is now raised for mismatched axes values (typeerror still raised for invalid types)

subject: np.linalg functions returning tuples
old: np.linalg functions like eig(), eigh(), qr(), slogdet(), svd() returned plain tuples
new: These functions now return NamedTuples
truth: Stop unpacking results from np.linalg.eig(), eigh(), qr(), slogdet(), and svd() as plain tuples; they now return NamedTuples which may break tuple unpacking patterns that rely on exact tuple type or indexing assumptions.
quote: ``np.linalg`` functions that return tuples now return namedtuples.

from_fact: np.linalg functions like eig(), eigh(), qr(), slogdet(), svd() returned plain tuples

subject: NDArrayOperatorsMixin
old: NDArrayOperatorsMixin did not specify __slots__
new: NDArrayOperatorsMixin now specifies it has no __slots__
truth: NDArrayOperatorsMixin now declares __slots__ = (), enabling subclasses to use __slots__ without conflict
quote: ``NDArrayOperatorsMixin`` specifies that it has no ``__slots__``

from_fact: ndarrayoperatorsmixin now specifies it has no __slots__

subject: np.ma.diff
old: np.ma.diff with prepend/append returned a MaskedArray without the mask
new: np.ma.diff with prepend/append now returns a MaskedArray with the input mask preserved
truth: np.ma.diff with prepend/append arguments now preserves the input mask instead of dropping it
quote: Fix ``np.ma.diff`` not preserving the mask when called with arguments prepend/append.

from_fact: np.ma.diff with prepend/append returned a maskedarray without the mask
np.ma.diff with prepend/append now returns a maskedarray with the input mask preserved

subject: np.ma.dot()
old: only worked for 2d arrays
new: now works for non-2d arrays as well
truth: np.ma.dot() now supports non-2d arrays, matching np.dot() behavior
quote: Now it works for non-2d arrays as well as ``np.dot()``.

from_fact: now works for non-2d arrays as well

subject: numpy.lib.recfunctions.structured_to_unstructured
old: always returned a copy
new: returns a view when stride between fields is constant
truth: structured_to_unstructured now returns a view (not a copy) when field stride is constant, for ndarray, memmap and recarray
quote: ``structured_to_unstructured`` now returns a view, if the stride between the

from_fact: returns a view when stride between fields is constant
